name: Braze Customer Engagement Platform
what_it_does: Enterprise customer engagement platform API for delivering highly personalized push, email, SMS, in-app, and webhook messages at scale with advanced segmentation and lifecycle orchestration.
use_cases: Trigger personalized push notifications from an AI agent when a user's tracked metric crosses a threshold (e.g., spend limit, activity goal)
Send transactional SMS messages with agent-generated personalized content to users who opt into SMS communication
Orchestrate multi-step engagement campaigns using Braze Canvas, initiated by agent-detected behavioral signals
Update user profile attributes and custom events in Braze from agent observations to power downstream segment-based campaigns
Track agent-driven conversion events to measure the downstream impact of AI recommendations on user behavior
not_for: Small teams or startups — Braze is enterprise-priced and requires significant setup; use Mailchimp, Customer.io, or Loops for smaller scale
Simple transactional email only — using Braze solely as an ESP is extreme overkill; use SendGrid or Postmark instead
Self-service or no-code teams — Braze has a steep learning curve and benefits from dedicated CRM/marketing engineering support
best_when: You are a large-scale consumer product with millions of users needing sophisticated personalization, segmentation, A/B testing, and cross-channel engagement orchestration.
avoid_when: Your user base is under 100K or you primarily need transactional notifications — the cost and complexity of Braze is not justified at small scale.

auth.notes: REST API keys are scoped with granular permissions (e.g., send messages only, export users only, update user data only). Keys are created per app group in the Braze dashboard with allowlisted IP ranges optional.

pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing based on monthly active users (MAU); typically starts at $30K-$60K/year for small enterprise; large enterprises pay hundreds of thousands annually
pricing.notes: No self-service sign-up — requires sales engagement. Pricing is MAU-based with channel add-ons. Some channel costs (SMS) are billed separately per message.

performance.rate_limits: Track user events: 100 requests/second per app group; send messages: varies by endpoint (typically 250K recipients/request); rate limit headers provided

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Braze API returns detailed error messages with specific field-level validation errors. Rate limit responses include headers indicating reset times. Errors are well-documented in the official API reference.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: The /messages/send endpoint accepts an optional broadcast flag but does not support idempotency keys. Agents triggering sends must implement deduplication externally to prevent duplicate message delivery.
known_agent_gotchas: External user IDs must match exactly between agent system and Braze — mismatches create duplicate user profiles that are expensive to merge and can corrupt segmentation data
Braze's rate limits are per app group, not per API key — multiple agents sharing one app group compete for the same rate limit budget
Canvas and campaign IDs are required for triggered sends — these must be created and activated in the Braze dashboard UI before agents can trigger them programmatically
User attribute updates are not immediately reflected in segments — Braze processes attribute updates asynchronously, so segment membership may lag by minutes for time-sensitive targeting
Braze data centers are region-specific (US-01 through US-08, EU-01, etc.) — agents must use the correct cluster-specific API endpoint or all requests will 404 silently

security_score_components.security_notes: Granular API key permissions and optional IP allowlisting are strong controls for an enterprise platform. SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliance. Data residency options for EU workloads.
